source_record.title: Developing SEMA4A-directed CAR T cells to overcome low BCMA antigen density in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: Translational and preclinical target exploration after BCMA CAR T relapse with low BCMA antigen density; public PubMed abstract and metadata used for this v0 public extraction.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source frames relapse with low-to-negative BCMA expression after BCMA CAR T therapy as a reason to investigate additional targets.

translation_safety_context.tumor_target_context: The source frames relapse with low-to-negative BCMA after BCMA CAR T as an alternate-target research context.
translation_safety_context.normal_tissue_context: Normal-tissue reactivity is not assessed in this target-need signal and must be reviewed separately.
translation_safety_context.model_context: This signal records post-BCMA CAR T relapse target-biology rationale, not a therapeutic model result.
translation_safety_context.clinical_translation_status: Target-biology rationale only; it does not establish intervention readiness.
translation_safety_context.trial_registry_context: No trial registry or protocol ID is captured in this v0 public extraction.
translation_safety_context.efficacy_claim_boundary: Does not show SEMA4A-directed CAR T efficacy in patients.
translation_safety_context.safety_claim_boundary: Does not assess SEMA4A normal-tissue safety.
translation_safety_context.actionability_boundary: Not a testing, treatment, sequencing, eligibility, or trial recommendation.

limitations: Does not estimate all low-BCMA relapse frequency from the public abstract alone.
Low target density is not the only possible relapse mechanism.
Clinical applicability requires trial and safety evidence.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports quantitative antigen-density profiling showing high SEMA4A molecules per cell where BCMA density is low in a post-BCMA CAR T relapse cohort.

translation_safety_context.tumor_target_context: The source reports SEMA4A antigen-density context where BCMA density is low; this extraction preserves the molecules-per-cell measurement language.
translation_safety_context.normal_tissue_context: Tumor antigen-density context is separate from normal-tissue reactivity and safety interpretation.
translation_safety_context.model_context: This is antigen-density profiling context, not a therapeutic model-efficacy result.
translation_safety_context.clinical_translation_status: Target-density support is used only for alternate-target research tracking.
translation_safety_context.trial_registry_context: No trial registry or protocol ID is captured in this v0 public extraction.
translation_safety_context.efficacy_claim_boundary: Candidate target density does not establish clinical efficacy or patient benefit.
translation_safety_context.safety_claim_boundary: Candidate tumor expression does not establish normal-tissue safety.
translation_safety_context.actionability_boundary: Not a testing, treatment, sequencing, eligibility, or trial recommendation.

limitations: Candidate target density does not establish therapeutic efficacy.
Requires full-methods review for cohort composition and assay details.
Target expression in tumor cells must be weighed against normal-tissue safety.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports generation and screening of SEMA4A-directed CAR T constructs with preclinical activity against multiple myeloma models, including BCMA-low contexts.

translation_safety_context.tumor_target_context: The activity signal is framed around multiple myeloma models, including BCMA-low contexts.
translation_safety_context.normal_tissue_context: Normal non-hematopoietic tissue reactivity is a separate safety-context field and is not the same as clinical safety.
translation_safety_context.model_context: The source reports generated and screened SEMA4A-directed CAR T constructs using expansion, cytokine release, cytotoxicity, and patient-derived BCMA-low tumor model contexts.
translation_safety_context.clinical_translation_status: The public source frames work toward phase 1 clinical translation, but this extraction does not record patient efficacy.
translation_safety_context.trial_registry_context: No trial registry or protocol ID is captured in this v0 public extraction; registry or protocol review is needed before any trial-readiness claim.
translation_safety_context.efficacy_claim_boundary: Preclinical activity is not clinical efficacy or patient benefit.
translation_safety_context.safety_claim_boundary: Preclinical and normal-tissue screens do not establish clinical safety.
translation_safety_context.actionability_boundary: Not a testing, treatment, sequencing, eligibility, or trial recommendation.

limitations: Preclinical activity does not establish clinical benefit.
Do not label this signal as clinical efficacy.
Phase 1 clinical readiness claims require trial registry and protocol review.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source highlights measurement fields needed for alternate-target work, including antigen density, normal-tissue reactivity, model context, and planned clinical translation.

translation_safety_context.tumor_target_context: Target expression on tumor cells is separate from normal-tissue reactivity and safety interpretation.
translation_safety_context.normal_tissue_context: Normal non-hematopoietic tissue reactivity is recorded as a required alternate-target safety-context field.
translation_safety_context.model_context: This signal records safety-field extraction guidance, not a full preclinical or clinical safety dataset.
translation_safety_context.clinical_translation_status: Safety package and protocol review are required before clinical translation can be assessed.
translation_safety_context.trial_registry_context: No trial registry or protocol ID is captured in this v0 public extraction.
translation_safety_context.efficacy_claim_boundary: No efficacy claim is supported by this safety-field signal.
translation_safety_context.safety_claim_boundary: Normal-tissue safety context does not establish clinical safety.
translation_safety_context.actionability_boundary: Not a testing, treatment, sequencing, eligibility, or trial recommendation.

limitations: Safety interpretation requires full preclinical package review and clinical protocol review.
Normal-tissue reactivity screening is safety context, not clinical safety.
Public abstract does not provide a full safety dataset.

what_this_supports: Low BCMA antigen density belongs in the BCMA antigen escape extraction field set.
Alternate-target strategies should capture target density, normal-tissue reactivity, model context, clinical translation status, and trial-registry or protocol status.
SEMA4A can be tracked as a candidate alternate-target research signal in post-BCMA relapse maps.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not prove SEMA4A-directed CAR T therapy works in patients.
It does not recommend a SEMA4A-directed clinical trial.
It does not establish that low BCMA density is present in all post-CAR T relapse.
It does not establish clinical safety for SEMA4A-directed therapy.
It does not replace safety, eligibility, or regulatory review.

review_notes: Built from PubMed ESummary and EFetch metadata/abstract for PMID 41072416.
Tracked as translational and preclinical signal extraction, not a clinical claim.
Issue #2 update separates target biology, preclinical activity, normal-tissue safety context, clinical translation status, trial-registry status, and claim boundaries.
